etymology.description: Originally the same character as 需 (xū, 'need') during the Han dynasty; diverged into a distinct form for 'soft; weak' -> phono-semantic development
etymology.interpretation: 耎 (ruǎn, 'soft; weak') is a phono-semantic compound that diverged from 需 (xū, 'need') during the Han dynasty, differentiating to express the notion of 'softness' or 'weakness'. It was specifically modified to convey a different meaning while retaining phonetic similarity.
